title: drosera septentrionalis
intro: Drosera septentrionalis, also known as Northern Sundew, is a carnivorous plant species that belongs to the Droseraceae family. It is native to the northern regions of Europe and Asia and is characterized by its unique ability to capture and digest insects to supplement its nutrient intake. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, including expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic compounds, which have been used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as skin conditions like wounds and acne. Traditionally, Drosera septentrionalis has been prepared as a herbal remedy in the form of infusions, tinctures, and salves to harness its health benefits and promote overall well-being.
